definition: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
usage_notes: Positive connotation — implies thoroughness and quality
Used for people, processes, and work products
Stronger than 'careful' — suggests near-obsessive attention to detail

etymology.origin_summary: From Latin 'meticulosus' — fearful, timid, from 'metus' (fear). Originally meant 'fearfully careful'; shifted to 'admirably careful'.

examples: Her meticulous record-keeping saved the audit from becoming a disaster.
The architect was meticulous about every measurement.

confusions.difference: 'Thorough' means complete; 'meticulous' means precise at a granular level.
'Pedantic' implies excessive focus on trivial details negatively; 'meticulous' is a positive trait.
